content.text: Prompt Builders
A prompt builder is a structured UI that lets users compose LLM inputs through
parameterised templates
rather than raw text boxes. The template contains
{{variables}}
; the builder renders controls for each variable and assembles the final prompt at submission time. This pattern is common in internal tooling, content pipelines, and no-code AI platforms.

attrs.alt: Prompt builder UI: variable controls sidebar, template editor with highlighted variables, and live compiled preview

content.text: Template Syntax and Parsing
Templates use
{{variable_name}}
placeholders. A parser extracts variable names from the template and generates the control sidebar dynamically:
React Component: TemplateEditor
State and Variable Controls
Variable Control Renderer
Live Preview with Token Count
Show the compiled prompt alongside a token estimate before submission. Token count uses the
chars / 4
heuristic (close enough for planning without a full tokeniser):
Template Version Management
Prompt templates are versioned in the backend. When a user saves a modified template, a new version is created immutably — old versions remain accessible for rollback and comparison:

// backend: template versioning API
# POST /v1/templates          → create new template (version 1)
# GET  /v1/templates/{id}     → get latest version
# GET  /v1/templates/{id}/v/{n} → get specific version
# POST /v1/templates/{id}/fork   → create new template from this version

# templates table (Postgres)
CREATE TABLE prompt_templates (
    id          UUID    PRIMARY KEY DEFAULT gen_random_uuid(),
    name        TEXT    NOT NULL,
    system      TEXT    NOT NULL,
    user_turn   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    variables   JSONB   NOT NULL,
    version     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
    created_by  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    created_at  TIMESTAMPTZ NOT NULL DEFAULT now(),
    UNIQUE (id, version)
);
Diff View Between Template Versions
Few-Shot Example Manager
Allow users to attach conversation examples to a template. Examples are stored as
message pairs
and injected before the live user turn:
Common Pitfalls
Unescaped user input in templates:
if userText contains {{something}}, it will be treated as a variable placeholder. Escape user-supplied content before embedding in templates.
Token overruns:
few-shot examples can add hundreds of tokens per example. Show a token meter and warn when combined prompt + examples exceed the model's context limit.
No variable validation:
missing or empty variables produce incomplete prompts silently. Validate that all required variables are filled before enabling the Run button.
Template state not persisted across navigation:
if a user fills in all variables and accidentally navigates away, their work is lost. Auto-save values to localStorage or URL search params.

The prompt builder is the interface between a product's domain knowledge (captured in templates) and the user's specific intent (captured in variable values). Keep the variable controls simple — dropdowns and toggles beat free-text for structured parameters. Reserve the free-text template editor for power users who need to adjust the system prompt directly.
